data.body: Root-caused via hypothesis-driven debugging (isolated fresh-install repro). VERDICT: environment-only artifact, NOT a source defect. On a clean venv from current origin/main (bf489e508), tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py passes (10 passed) and full collection is clean (4316 collected, 0 errors). The imported symbols all resolve: _normalize_comment_body is defined at rebar_reconciler/outbound_comments.py:119 and re-exported by outbound_differ.py:38. The reported ModuleNotFoundError only reproduces on a worktree/editable-install predating commit 3d8be7fec (2026-06-29), which CREATED outbound_comments.py in the per-concern differ split — such a stale tree has the rebar_reconciler package (so 'import rebar_reconciler' works) but lacks the outbound_comments submodule (so the dotted import fails). Resolution: already fixed on main by that split; no behavior change needed. Added a regression guard (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_outbound_comments_split_import.py) that imports outbound_differ and asserts the re-exported comment-split names resolve — verified teeth: renaming outbound_comments.py away reproduces the exact ModuleNotFoundError (RED), present = GREEN. This pins the split so a real future removal/rename fails loudly here instead of resurfacing as a confusing collection error.
